content: I agree about the disclosure. It would calm a lot of doubts.

Disclosure is a very uphill battle for companies with CN audiences.

Witchhunting of employees isn't that uncommon of an occurrence, that's why a lot of smaller Cn games don't even credit Cn voice actors.

It's always a question if people will even believe when told "no we don't use ai" and ask to credit the artist.

Only then for the artist to get bombarded.

I don't see this situation getting majorly better from disclosure of most kinds. Henceforth why it's ignored in the first place.

It's more of a responsibility of the person asking the question, to learn to figure it out... welp, if you want to question, and judge it, rather then consume/just support the fight against it.

Hating Ai art is more then fine.

 After all, as much as we don't want it. Ai slop is part of the world.

Reminded I'm pretty sure reverse 1999 still has no in-game voice actor credits for any VA's

Albeit I'm not sure. It didn't used to on launch.

content: or .... you can keep to

关于是否使用AI这个问题，让我和大家分享一些开发背后的故事吧。

众所周知，B.I.A.V.其实是个极小规模、预算非常有限的开发团队，我们的开发过程从来都不是一帆风顺。从2019年立项，到2024年1.0结局发布，整整五年间团队经历了剧烈的变动，除了美术，几乎每个岗位都换过两到三轮成员。包括我自己，也不是项目最初的发起人，而是2021年末，因为对这个项目的热爱才加入，随后在游戏研发进度受阻之际又成为了第二任制作人，重新设计玩法、组建设计团队。

由于重新立项，任务量极大、预算又极其有限，理智上其实我应该优先缩减美术团队以节约时间，但我还是选择把大部分预算投入在美术上，其他地方靠节省和超负荷工作支撑。所以大家可以看到，游戏早期上线时确实有很多不够完善的地方。

为什么坚持这样做？其实很简单，我和团队里许多人，都是被2020年美术团队最初的PV所打动，才愿意投入这款几乎没人有开发经验的游戏项目。而我的唯一目标，就是用自己的努力帮助这些优秀的艺术家，把多年来的心血真正变成游戏，绝不让他们的付出白费。

2023年底游戏终于上线，但因为类型新颖、宣传资源有限，前半年基本入不敷出，不得不面临项目关停、团队解散的窘境。在最后的资金压力下，我也请求大家一同把结局做完。最后两章主线的开发几乎每周都有人完成工作后离开，留下来的成员，包括最早的美术，真的是用尽全力在坚持。那段时间对我来说非常煎熬。

我们知晓这可能是最后的更新，就希望能让游戏被更多人看到和游玩。然而又没有预算去做高质量的人工本地化，所以我们只能在2024年秋季上线Steam，并借助AI模型，几乎零人工校对地完成了多语言翻译。我明白AI翻译存在很多缺陷，名词错乱、语境不顺、缺乏人工翻译的审美和诗意。但那时还有很多玩家只能靠屏幕翻译凑合游玩，所以我觉得，这样的AI翻译至少让更多人看得懂。为了让大家明确地知道这一点，我们在语言选项里特意标示了GPT翻译。

然而也正是凭借Steam和AI翻译的机会，让游戏被更多人关注，最终遇到了日本发行商Altplus。在他们和全球玩家的支持下，我们有幸开启了2.0的开发，这对我们来说简直就是一次“复活”。可惜，这一路很多曾经的成员，尤其是原美术团队，已经离开了。当我想向他们分享这份喜悦时，他们已经不在身边。祝福他们今后在更大的项目里收获成功，我们也正在努力重建并提升美术团队，努力不辜负大家的期待。

回到AI的问题。  

必须承认，随着AI技术兴起和研究学习的需要，我们的确在2023年开始尝试过用AI进行头脑风暴、创意讨论阶段来尝试提高效率，而这部分内容在整个游戏中数量极其有限，即便个别有AI的辅助，人工艺术家仍然占据了绝对主要的创意工作。

我的看法是，目前AI还有大量无法取代人类艺术家的短板，不论在设计表达还是执行协作方面。但或许未来终会发生变化。如果有一天真的正式用到AI来生成内容，我们一定会像对待AI翻译一样，明确地、坦荡地告知各位玩家，而不是偷偷摸摸。

如果大家了解这些故事，就会明白为什么我们会用AI做翻译，因为这真的是在资源有限、让更多人能体验到游戏的唯一途径。

如果大家了解这些故事，就会发现我们有大量重要概念与角色资产早在2019-2021年就已创作，那个时候AI生成艺术还极不成熟远未被人知。而在2022-2024年我们主要是将游戏艺术真正在游戏中完成，动作、特效等等，这些内容目前还并没有成熟的技术。即便有，作为一个预算很小、总是在加班的团队我们也难以掌握这样的技术。许多作图上的瑕疵只是我们人类自身不断学习与成长的痕迹。


如果大家了解这些故事，就可以看到，游戏创作对于B.I.A.V.来说意味着什么。每个人在知晓会明天离开这个团队的情况下，也会抓紧时间尽力做好每一个内容……这个游戏对我们来说，不只是工作，而是生命的证明。过去如此，将来亦然。

谢谢大家的关心、理解与支持。

（GPT translated）

Regarding the question of whether we used AI, I’d like to share some behind-the-scenes stories from the development process.

As many of you know, B.I.A.V. is a very small team with extremely limited budget, and our development journey has been far from smooth. From the project’s initiation in 2019 to the 1.0 ending release in 2024, the team has experienced drastic changes—except for the art team, almost every position saw two or even three different groups of people over these five years. I myself was not part of the original founders; I joined at the end of 2021 because of my passion for this project. When development stalled, I became the second producer, redesigned the gameplay, and reassembled the design team.

Due to reworking the game from scratch, the workload became immense while the budget remained extremely tight. Rationally, I should have prioritized reducing the size of the art team to save time, but I still chose to allocate most of our resources to the art team, compensating elsewhere through cost-cutting and overwork. As a result, you can see that the game’s early release had many imperfections.

Why did I insist on doing this? It’s simple: both I and many teammates were drawn in by the very first PV released by the art team in 2020. We joined what was then a team with almost no prior game development experience, motivated by admiration for these artists. My only goal was to help these talented creators turn their years of hard work into a full game—I didn’t want their efforts to go to waste.

By the end of 2023, we finally released the game, but because its style was novel and we lacked marketing resources, we incurred heavy losses in the first half year and had to face possible shutdown and team disbandment. Under this last bit of financial pressure, I asked everyone to come together to finish the final chapters. During development of those last two arcs, almost every week, someone would finish their last task and leave; those who remained, including some of our original artists, truly gave it everything they had. That period was incredibly tough for me.

Knowing these could be our last updates, we hoped our game could reach and be enjoyed by as many people as possible. Without funds for high-quality manual localization, in autumn 2024 we launched on Steam and used AI models—almost entirely without manual review—to provide multilingual translations. I understand that AI translations have many issues: inconsistent terminology, awkward context, and a lack of the artistry and poetry that human translators can provide. But at the time, many players had to rely on screen translators, which was even worse, so I thought at least with AI translation more people could understand the story. To make this clear, we explicitly marked “GPT translation” in the language options.

Yet, it was precisely thanks to opportunities brought by Steam and AI translation that our game reached more people, and we were finally approached by the Japanese publisher Altplus. With their support, and that of players around the world, we were lucky enough to begin development on version 2.0—a true revival for us. Unfortunately, many team members, especially from the original art team, had already left by then. By the time I wanted to share this joy with them, they were no longer by my side. I wish them every success on larger projects in the future, and meanwhile we are working hard to rebuild and improve our art team, so as not to let anyone down.

Back to the AI question:

It’s true that, with the rise of AI technology and out of a need to keep up with technical advancements, we began to experiment in 2023 with AI for brainstorming or idea refinement, aiming to boost efficiency during concept discussions. However, the amount of AI-assisted content in the game is extremely limited; even when used, human artists remain absolutely central to all creative work.

Personally, I believe AI still has many shortcomings that can’t replace human artists, whether in design, expression, or collaborative execution. That may change in the future. If the day ever comes when we use AI to generate official content, we will absolutely disclose it to players—openly and clearly—just as we do with AI translation, and never secretly.

If you understand this history, you’ll see why we opted for AI translation: limited resources meant it was the only way to let more people experience the game.

If you understand our story, you’ll also realize that many of our core concepts and character assets were created as early as 2019–2021, when AI-generated art was still immature and little-known. Most of our later work (2022–2024) was about finishing art implementation, animations, effects, etc.—areas where today’s technology is still not fully developed. Even if such tech exists, as a small, perpetually overtime team, we simply don’t have access to it. Many art flaws you see simply reflect our human process of learning and growth.

If you know all this, you’ll see what game development means to B.I.A.V.: for everyone on the team, even when we knew we might be leaving tomorrow, we tried our hardest to complete every part of the game. For us, this project isn't just a job, but proof of life itself. It was true in the past, and will be in the future.

Thank you for your concern, understanding, and support.
@everyone
